title: Stale stream timeout does not trigger fallback_providers chain
summary:
### Problem

When the primary provider becomes unresponsive during streaming (no chunks delivered within the stale timeout), Hermes kills the connection and retries with the **same** provider. It does **not** activate the `fallback_providers` chain.

This means that if a provider is alive but unresp

title: 10 minutes http client timeout is too low when image generation is done on CPU
summary:
### What is the issue?

[This line](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/x/imagegen/server.go#L58) sets the 10 minute timeout regardless of the hardware.

This is maybe fine on a GPU, but is certainly too low for a CPU.
The timeout should be at least an hour or more when CPU is used.

title: [PR] feat: Add GitHub Copilot provider
summary:
## Description

Adds GitHub Copilot as a first-class LLM provider in Bifrost. The Copilot provider uses a two-layer OAuth flow: device-code grant to obtain a GitHub token, then exchanges it for a Copilot API token. Supports Chat Completions, Responses API (with text format mapping), streaming, and d

title: [PR] Canary Token Guardrail: a native LangChain4j guardrail feature to detect and remediate LLM07:2025 System Prompt Leakage
summary:
## Issue
Closes #4587 

## Change
Implements canary token-based detection mechanism to identify and mitigate prompt injection attacks (OWASP LLM07). Injects unique tokens into prompts and monitors for their appearance in model outputs to detect potential injection attempts.

title: [PR] feat(ai): every subprocess exposes /task/data via a shared web server
summary:
## Summary

Hoist the subprocess `WebServer` out of `webhook` / `telegram` source
nodes and into `node.py`, so **every** subprocess listens on its
`--data_port` for DAP traffic regardless of pipeline shape. Today the
listener only exists if the pipeline happens to contain a webhook /
chat / dr

title: [PR] test(e2e): execute real shell assertions; delete dry-run, --validate-only, and the bash runner
summary:
## Why

Removing `--dry-run` and plan-mode execution from the E2E runner: they let a coding agent ship work behind false-green test runs. The TS runner now has **one execution mode: live.** No flag, env var, helper, or branch in any production path bypasses real assertion execution.

title: [PR] Llmflux using api
summary:
Adds a new serving mode to LLMFlux that lets users start a model as a long-running OpenAI-compatible endpoint on a compute node, without managing SLURM themselves.

title: [PR] Validate and apply sanitized config before persisting to prevent invalid provider persistence
